meta.title: Pointint — Your Point, Your Tint.
meta.description: Turn your image into a custom Windows cursor. The easiest way to make your own cursor.

home.title: Pointint
home.description: Turn your image into a custom Windows cursor.

upload.dropOrClick: Drop image or click to upload

studio.title: Studio
studio.mobileMessage: Cursor creation works best on desktop. Open this page on a computer to start making your cursor.

panel.name: Name
panel.nameInvalidChars: cannot be used in file names
panel.fillSquareSub: Fills the frame and may crop edges

guide.title: Apply your cursor
guide.step3: Open Settings → Mouse → "Additional mouse settings" → Pointers tab
guide.step4: Select "Pointint" from the Scheme dropdown → OK

simulation.instruction: Move your cursor around this area
simulation.sampleText: This is selectable text. Hover here to see your cursor as a text selection cursor. Try selecting some of this text to see how it feels in real use.
simulation.sampleLink: This is a clickable link — hover to preview

landing.step1Sub: PNG, JPG, or WebP — background removed automatically
landing.step2Sub: Position, resize, and set your hotspot. Preview at 32, 48, or 64px
landing.step3Sub: Get your .cur file with one-click Windows installer
landing.faq1Q: How do I make a custom Windows cursor?
landing.faq1A: Upload an image to Pointint, edit the position and hotspot, simulate how it looks, and download a .cur file with a one-click installer.
landing.faq2A: PNG, JPG, and WebP. Pointint automatically removes the background and converts your image.
landing.faq3A: Yes. Upload, edit, simulate, and download — all free, no account required.
landing.faq4Q: How do I apply the cursor to Windows?
landing.faq4A: Unzip the downloaded file, right-click install.inf, select "Install", then choose "Pointint" in Mouse Settings > Pointers.
landing.faq5A: The exact pixel where your click registers. Pointint lets you set it visually with a drag tool.
landing.faq6A: Animated cursor (.ani) support is coming soon.
